key_themes: Q1 2026 revenue beat overshadowed by lowered full-year EPS guidance
Dilution impact ($0.20/share) from Exact Sciences cancer diagnostics acquisition
Strong Medical Devices growth contrasted with margin pressure and nutrition weakness
Stock decline to near 52-week lows (~6% drop post-earnings)
Long-term optimism on pipeline acceleration and H2 2026 growth despite transition year
summary: Abbott Laboratories (ABT) delivered a solid Q1 2026 with revenue of $11.16B (beating estimates) and adjusted EPS of $1.15 (in line), driven by strong Medical Devices performance. However, the company cut its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $5.38–$5.58 (from $5.55–$5.80) citing $0.20 dilution from the completed Exact Sciences acquisition, while maintaining comparable sales growth of 6.5–7.5%. This triggered a sharp ~6% stock drop to near 52-week lows around $95–97, with several analyst price target reductions, though consensus remains Buy with ~$121 average target implying significant upside. Investors see it as a transitional year with near-term pressure but long-term tailwinds from the cancer diagnostics entry, new product pipeline, and expected H2 acceleration.[[1]](https://www.investors.com/news/technology/abbott-laboratories-stock-abbott-laboratories-earnings-q1-2026/)[[2]](https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/abbott-first-quarter-results-beat-cancer-diagnostics-medical-device-strength-2026-04-16/)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/healthcare/articles/abbott-laboratories-nyse-abt-q1-115900320.html)
sources: 20+ news articles on Q1 2026 earnings and guidance update (Investor's Business Daily, Reuters, Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, etc.), 15+ recent X posts discussing earnings reaction, stock price action, and quality-at-lows lists
